# Inkwell — Forkable Agent-First Publishing Framework

> Astro + Cloudflare framework for content, commerce, dashboards, and MCP-enabled agent workflows.

## What Inkwell Provides

- Config-driven site identity, theme, analytics, SEO, and feature flags
- Markdown/MDX publishing with optional agent inbox and API publishing
- Cloudflare Worker API for analytics, publishing, dashboard, MCP, and plugin routes
- Plugin architecture for content, commerce, auth, dashboard, media, SEO, feedback, and automation
- Hexagonal ports for database, session, content, storage, graph, agent, bus, memory, economy, media, SEO, and feedback adapters
- `create-inkwell` scaffold for new forks

## Common Routes

- `/` — Framework homepage or fork landing page
- `/blog` — Published articles
- `/docs` — Documentation collection when present
- `/products` — Product collection when present
- `/tools` — Tool and integration collection when present
- `/topics` — Topic hubs when present
- `/search` — Static Pagefind search
- `/connect` — MCP connection helper
- `/dashboard` — Optional dashboard shell
- `/sitemap.xml` — Sitemap
- `/rss.xml` — RSS feed

## Agent Interfaces

Deploy the Worker and connect an MCP-compatible client to:

- `POST /mcp`
- `POST /api/publish`
- `GET /api/dashboard`
- `GET /api/stats/:slug`

Use `PUBLISH_TOKEN`, `INKWELL_MCP_TOKEN`, and Worker environment variables from `.env.example` / `workers/inkwell-api/.env.example`.

## Technical Stack

- Astro
- React islands
- Cloudflare Workers
- Hono
- D1, KV, R2
- Pagefind
- Model Context Protocol

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/Mumega-com/inkwell
- Setup: `npx create-inkwell my-site`
- Config: `inkwell.config.ts`
